payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields are: token counts (input/output), provider/model IDs, estimated price, and latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95). These enable cost attribution, performance benchmarking, and reproducible evaluation.
payload.decision: Provide a structured explanation of required evaluation accounting fields for model inference: token counts, provider/model IDs, estimated price, and latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95).

entity_resolution.reason: All entities are explicitly defined in context; no external resolution needed.

memory_cards.statement: Token counts (input and output) are required to measure computational usage and cost basis for model inference.
Provider and model IDs are required to uniquely identify the service and specific model version used for accurate attribution and reproducibility.
Estimated price is required to quantify the financial cost of inference based on token usage and provider pricing models.
Latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95) are required to characterize response time distribution and assess performance under typical and tail-load conditions.

details: No ambiguity introduced; each field's purpose is clearly tied to evaluation integrity.
No superseded or deleted facts presented.
